methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $71M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Pixxel is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $71M to date. The valuation analysis draws on two applicable methodologies: stage-progression benchmarking and peer-median positioning within a universe of four same-stage, same-sector private companies (Aalyria, Stoke Space, Hubble Network, and Sift), alongside reference to five public space and aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) that trade at a median 45.3x P/S.

The stage-progression method, applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $135M. The peer-median approach across the four private comparables yields $700M, with the sample ranging from $274M (Sift) to $1.3B (Aalyria). These methods reflect the wide dispersion common in early-stage space ventures, where technology maturity and contractual validation drive outsized valuation spreads.

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a central estimate of $417M, bounded by a low of $115M and a high of $805M. The wide band reflects genuine uncertainty around Pixxel's technology readiness relative to peers and the absence of disclosed revenue data, which precludes public-comp anchoring. Key variables determining position within this range include satellite payload performance, customer contracts, and go-to-market traction relative to the Aalyria and Stoke Space cohort.
